anti_claim: This explanation describes project-local public state only. It does not authorize source mutation, release, provider calls, private-data equivalence, or global doctrine promotion.
macro_analogue: navigation option surface + mission transaction + evidence membrane

pattern_surface.projection_rule: Catalog role observations become public pattern rows; routes carry pattern_refs that must resolve against local .microcosm/patterns.json before explanation.

standard.claim: Project-local JSON is the contract; markdown and browser views are projections.
standard.title: JSON Contract, Markdown Projection

standard.claim: Explanations must derive from local state files instead of hand-authored proof prose.
standard.title: Substrate-Derived Projection

standard.claim: Generated route, graph, explanation, and evidence views must expose lineage and anti-claims.
standard.title: Projection Lineage, Not Authority

standard.claim: A work run records definition, execution, contracts, state history, and closeout without mutating source.
standard.title: Reversible Work Transaction

standard.claim: Events and receipts record what happened; they are drilldown evidence, not the cockpit.
standard.title: Evidence As Black-Box Recorder

standard.claim: Closeout records next local actions and residuals without promoting global doctrine.
standard.title: Assimilation Without Promotion

standard.claim: Formal-math readiness surfaces may report closed premise, tactic, route, and context availability, but they must not claim theorem proof, Mathlib, provider, or Lean/Lake execution authority.
standard.title: Formal Readiness, Not Proof Authority

standard.claim: Corpus readiness surfaces must make Mathlib absence explicit and block dependent consumers before proof work.
standard.title: Corpus Readiness Mathlib Absence Gate

standard.claim: Formal-math retrieval surfaces may rank public synthetic premises, but they must not claim theorem proof or Lean/Lake authority.
standard.title: Formal Retrieval, Not Proof Authority

standard.claim: Provider context recipes may budget public sections and deliverable routes, but they must not call providers, expose proof bodies, or include oracle-only truth-side material.
standard.title: Provider Context Recipe Budget, No Provider Authority

standard.claim: Strategy selection is a pre-oracle metadata hypothesis and retrieval lens; it cannot carry proof bodies, oracle labels, provider output, test tuning, or release authority.
standard.title: Mathematical Strategy Atlas, Pre-Oracle Hypothesis Boundary

standard.claim: Tactic availability is an environment-scoped probe artifact; Mathlib-dependent tactics cannot be marked available without a passing Mathlib import probe, and unprobed tactics cannot be consumed downstream.
standard.title: Tactic Portfolio Availability, Environment Boundary

standard.claim: Target-shape tactic routing admits or blocks public tactic candidates before execution; it must not claim Lean proof authority, provider output, post-execution routing, or release operations.
standard.title: Target Shape Tactic Routing, No Proof Authority

standard.claim: Lean/Lake may run only for the tiny public witness fixture, with redacted receipts and no Mathlib or provider authority.
standard.title: Formal Lean Witness, Bounded Authority

standard.claim: Prediction reconciliation is synthetic fixture reasoning only; it must not imply trading, financial advice, live market data, provider authority, performance claims, publication, or release.
standard.title: Prediction Oracle Reconciliation, No Advice Authority

standard.claim: Ring-2 precision/recall metrics explain retrieval quality for a synthetic fixture; they must not claim theorem proof, benchmark performance, provider authority, test-tuned retrieval, or release operations.
standard.title: Ring-2 Retrieval Precision/Recall, No Proof Authority

standard.claim: Standards meta diagnostics may summarize organ-standard-runtime-receipt coverage, but must not become registry source authority or authorize release, provider calls, secret export, trading advice, or whole-system correctness.
standard.title: Standards Meta Diagnostics, Projection Only

standard.claim: Cold-reader route maps may make the public first-run path explicit with commands, docs refs, and receipt refs, but must not become route registry authority or authorize release, provider calls, secret export, trading advice, source mutation, or whole-system correctness.
standard.title: Cold-Reader Route Map, Projection Only

standard.claim: Lean/Std premise indexes may expose closed public metadata with Init source refs, namespace coverage, split eligibility, and retrieval terms, but must not expose Mathlib imports, proof bodies, oracle-needed ids, test split tuning, provider calls, or release authority.
standard.title: Lean/Std Premise Index, Closed Metadata Only

standard.claim: Verifier feedback may drive repair and curriculum metadata only when trace events, verifier classes, and cold rerun receipts are present.
standard.title: Verifier Trace Repair Is Not Proof Authority

standard.claim: Proof-language claims must resolve to public evidence-cell metadata with source-anchor refs before they can be stated as evidence-cell-present claims.
standard.title: Evidence Cell Anchors Are Not Theorem Authority

standard.claim: Lean-accepted proof receipts that use a known library symbol outside the allowed premise set must be quarantined as undeclared library priors, unless an explicit cited-unallowed premise already makes it a premise-budget violation.
standard.title: Undeclared Library Priors Are Quarantined Separately From Premise Budgets

title: Add behavior-test route
why_this_route_exists: The catalog classified project files, pattern observations mapped those roles to repo-shape signals, and the route primitive projected a reversible next action from those grounded refs.

result.summary: Simulated a governed local transaction over project catalog state.

state_history.note: Work transaction record created from a route snapshot.
Route selected for deterministic local simulation.
Source mutation is not authorized; run step will simulate governance only.
Executed deterministic project-local simulation over route snapshot.
Closed with generated evidence and no source mutation.
